speaker: Landlord
en: Hello?

speaker: Student
en: Oh, hello.

speaker: Student
en: I wanted to enquire about the house you're going to let out.

speaker: Student
en: Is it still available?

speaker: Landlord
en: Yes.

speaker: Landlord
en: May I ask where you heard about it?

speaker: Landlord
en: Was it in an advert?

speaker: Student
en: Right, it was.

speaker: Landlord
en: OK, what do you want to know?

speaker: Student
en: Well, I'm going to share the house with two friends.

speaker: Student
en: We've been staying on campus, but we're going to graduate, so we need to find a house as soon as possible.

speaker: Student
en: Anyway, could you tell me the exact date when we can move in?

speaker: Landlord
en: Well, the contract with my current tenant is due next week, on May 3rd.

speaker: Landlord
en: And I need a day to clean and check the things in the house, so I think the house will be ready on 5th May.

speaker: Landlord
en: Is that OK?

speaker: Student
en: Yes, that will be fine.

speaker: Student
en: And what we're most concerned about is the rent.

speaker: Student
en: We'd like something around 500 dollars a month for each of us, but we could go up to 600 dollars if we have to.

speaker: Student
en: But we can't go beyond that.

speaker: Landlord
en: You are lucky – the rent will be 1,700 dollars in total, which is within your budget.

speaker: Landlord
en: You'll need to pay monthly to me, by cash or credit card, I don't mind.

speaker: Landlord
en: And you'll have to pay me 1,500 dollars as a deposit before you move in.

speaker: Landlord
en: The deposit is as insurance, in case you break something.

speaker: Student
en: OK, that's reasonable.

speaker: Landlord
en: Oh, I nearly forgot.

speaker: Landlord
en: One important thing is that you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.

speaker: Landlord
en: This will charge you 15 dollars.

speaker: Student
en: No problem.

speaker: Student
en: How about the facilities?

speaker: Landlord
en: First there are three bedrooms combined with two bathrooms.

speaker: Landlord
en: Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.

speaker: Landlord
en: By the way, do you cook yourselves?

speaker: Student
en: Yeah, we like cooking.

speaker: Student
en: Do we have to buy our own cookers?

speaker: Landlord
en: Basic facilities for cooking are available, but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.

speaker: Student
en: I think the basic facilities are enough for us.

speaker: Student
en: Is there any place we can park?

speaker: Student
en: As one of my friends has a private car.

speaker: Landlord
en: Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.

speaker: Landlord
en: It's very quiet for you to concentrate.

speaker: Student
en: It sounds so special!

speaker: Landlord
en: There are some other utilities I have to tell you.

speaker: Landlord
en: There is a garden in front of the house, in which you will find some interesting plants and beautiful flowers.

speaker: Student
en: Do we have to take care of these plants and flowers?

speaker: Landlord
en: Well, you don't have to worry about that.

speaker: Landlord
en: There will be a professional gardener to do the landscaping in the garden regularly.

speaker: Landlord
en: But you do need to help water the grass once a week.

speaker: Landlord
en: Is it a problem for you?

speaker: Student
en: Of course not.

speaker: Student
en: We'd like to do that.

speaker: Landlord
en: Another thing is, in this district every household is arranged with a cleaner responsible for cleaning up the rubbish and recycling, but this service will cost you 15 dollars every month.

speaker: Student
en: What other bills do we have to pay?

speaker: Landlord
en: Bills for electricity, water and gas will also be covered by yourselves.

speaker: Student
en: OK.

speaker: Student
en: One more question, is air-conditioning available in the house?

speaker: Landlord
en: If you mean central air-conditioning, I'm afraid there isn't at the moment, but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.

speaker: Student
en: That will be fine.

speaker: Student
en: Anyway, I think it's a good place to live in.

speaker: Student
en: Can we go and see the house, please?

speaker: Landlord
en: Yes, of course.

speaker: Student
en: May I have your name and phone number?

speaker: Landlord
en: Yes, I'm Sam Dressler.

speaker: Landlord
en: That's S-A-M, D-R-E-double S-L-E-R.

speaker: Student
en: OK, Sam Dressler, I've got that down.

speaker: Landlord
en: And my phone number is 6753 4571.

speaker: Student
en: Thank you very much.

speaker: Student
en: I'll contact you after discussing it with my two friends.

speaker: Landlord
en: No problem.

speaker: Landlord
en: Bye!

speaker: Student
en: Bye!
